metadata.summary: Integrate OpenAI Chat Completions API with proper authentication, model selection, error handling, and production patterns. Use async for batch processing and tiktoken for token counting.

content.title: OpenAI API Integration
content.summary: OpenAI API provides access to GPT-4, GPT-4 Turbo, GPT-4o, and other models for text generation, embeddings, image generation, and more. This methodology covers authentication, model selection, request handling, and production-ready integration patterns.

why.p: OpenAI's API provides state-of-the-art language capabilities and a well-documented, production-grade interface. The official Python and JavaScript SDKs handle authentication, error recovery, and async execution patterns that are critical for building scalable applications. Integrating the API correctly prevents common failures like truncated responses, authentication errors, and rate limiting issues.

list.item: Building conversational AI applications
Text generation, summarization, translation
Code generation and analysis
Content moderation and classification
When you need state-of-the-art language capabilities
Production applications requiring high reliability
The task fits a smaller model (Claude Haiku, GPT-4o-mini) — always start with the cheapest model that meets quality bar
You need 1M+ context — Gemini 1.5 Pro is the right tool; OpenAI's max is 128K
The project policy requires on-premise or self-hosted inference — use Ollama or a local model
The response format is unknown/flexible — structured output requires knowing the schema up front

section.name: models-hierarchy
section.p: Models available as of 2024-2025. Capabilities and costs vary; select based on task complexity and budget.

rule.statement: Model selection MUST match task complexity: gpt-4o-mini for simple tasks and cost optimization, gpt-4o for general tasks with multimodal support, gpt-4-turbo for complex reasoning, gpt-4 for legacy compatibility only.
rule.rationale: Model cost and capability differ significantly; selecting the wrong model wastes budget (using gpt-4-turbo for simple tasks) or produces poor results (using gpt-4o-mini for complex reasoning). Start with the cheapest option and escalate only if quality tests fail.

list.item: gpt-4o | 128K context | Multimodal, general tasks | Medium cost
gpt-4o-mini | 128K context | Fast, cost-effective | Low cost
gpt-4-turbo | 128K context | Complex reasoning | High cost
gpt-4 | 8K/32K context | Legacy support | High cost
gpt-3.5-turbo | 16K context | Simple tasks, high volume | Very low cost

section.name: api-structure
section.p: OpenAI's REST API exposes multiple endpoints for different capabilities.

list.item: POST https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions
POST https://api.openai.com/v1/embeddings
POST https://api.openai.com/v1/images/generations
POST https://api.openai.com/v1/audio/transcriptions

section.name: basic-setup
section.p: Initialize the OpenAI client and make basic completion requests.

example.title: Client initialization and simple completion
example.code:
from openai import OpenAI import os
# Initialize client client = OpenAI(api_key=os.environ.get("OPENAI_API_KEY"))
# Simple completion response = client.chat.completions.create( model="gpt-4o", messages=[ {"role": "system", "content": "You are a helpful assistant."}, {"role": "user", "content": "Explain quantum computing in simple terms."} ], temperature=0.7, max_tokens=500 )
print(response.choices[0].message.content)

section.name: streaming-responses
section.p: Stream responses for better user experience in interactive applications.

example.title: Streaming response implementation

section.name: async-implementation
section.p: Use AsyncOpenAI for concurrent batch processing of multiple prompts.

example.title: Async completion and batch processing
example.code:
import asyncio from openai import AsyncOpenAI
async_client = AsyncOpenAI()
async def async_completion(prompt: str) -> str: """Async completion for concurrent requests.""" response = await async_client.chat.completions.create( model="gpt-4o-mini", messages=[{"role": "user", "content": prompt}] ) return response.choices[0].message.content
async def batch_completions(prompts: list[str]) -> list[str]: """Process multiple prompts concurrently.""" tasks = [async_completion(p) for p in prompts] return await asyncio.gather(*tasks)

section.name: structured-output-json-mode
section.p: Extract structured data using JSON mode with Pydantic validation.

example.title: Structured output with JSON mode

section.name: error-handling-and-retries
section.p: Implement robust error handling with exponential backoff retry logic.

example.title: Error handling with tenacity retry decorator

section.name: token-counting
section.p: Count tokens before sending requests to estimate cost and detect context overflow.

example.title: Token counting and cost estimation

rule.statement: Always count tokens before sending a request and validate that (prompt_tokens + max_tokens) does not exceed the model's context limit; silently truncated responses (finish_reason == "length") cause data loss in production.
rule.rationale: When the response is truncated, the model stops at the context boundary without signaling that the output is incomplete. Agents that do not check `finish_reason` produce incomplete results that are hard to detect in production.

section.name: production-service-class
section.p: Wrap OpenAI API calls in a production-ready service class with logging and error handling.

example.title: Production-ready OpenAI service wrapper

list.item: API Key Management: Never hardcode API keys; use environment variables or secret managers; rotate keys periodically
Cost Control: Set usage limits in OpenAI dashboard; count tokens before sending large requests; use appropriate model for task complexity
Rate Limiting: Implement exponential backoff; use async for batch processing; consider request queuing for high volume
Prompt Engineering: Use system messages for consistent behavior; be specific about output format; include examples for complex tasks
Monitoring: Log all API calls with token usage; track latency and error rates; set up alerts for unusual patterns
Token Overflow: Not checking if prompt + max_tokens exceeds context limit
Ignoring Finish Reason: Not handling "length" finish reason (truncated output)
Blocking Calls: Using sync API in async applications
No Timeout: Requests hanging indefinitely
Hardcoded Prompts: Difficulty updating prompts in production
Missing Error Handling: API errors crashing applications

section.name: agent-integration
section.p: A subagent integrating OpenAI should initialize the `OpenAI` or `AsyncOpenAI` client from the environment, wrap calls with exponential backoff using `tenacity`, count tokens with `tiktoken` before sending to catch context overflow, and always check `finish_reason` in the response. For batch operations, the subagent should use `AsyncOpenAI` with `asyncio.gather` and a concurrency semaphore to stay within rate limits. Structured output should use `client.beta.chat.completions.parse` with Pydantic models for guaranteed schema compliance.

list.item: Use AsyncOpenAI to process {count} prompts concurrently (max {concurrency} at a time).
Model: gpt-4o-mini. Return structured JSON per prompt matching schema: {schema}.
Log token usage per request. Handle RateLimitError with exponential backoff (max 3 retries).
Extract structured data from the text using response_format=Person (Pydantic model).
Validate the parsed object. If parsing fails, return {"error": "parse_failed", "raw": "<response>"}.
`message.tool_calls` is `None` (not `[]`) when no tools are called; always use `if message.tool_calls:` not `if message.tool_calls is not None and len(message.tool_calls) > 0:`
Parallel tool calls: the model may call multiple tools in one response; agents that only handle the first `tool_call` silently drop the rest
`response_format={"type": "json_object"}` requires the word "json" to appear somewhere in the prompt or system message — otherwise the model ignores the format instruction
Human checkpoint before switching from `gpt-4o-mini` to `gpt-4o` in production; the cost jump is significant and should be justified by documented quality test results
Organization-level rate limits are shared across all API keys; multiple subagents running concurrently can exhaust the org's TPM limit even if each individual agent is well-behaved
`json_object` mode does not guarantee a specific schema — the LLM may omit fields or add extras; always validate with Pydantic
`AsyncOpenAI` does not work inside Jupyter notebooks without `nest_asyncio` — the event loop is already running
Rate limits are tier-dependent; newly created API keys start at Tier 1 (low RPM/TPM) and must be upgraded manually
The `openai` SDK v1 (>=1.0) is incompatible with code written for v0.x; `openai.ChatCompletion.create` vs. `client.chat.completions.create` — check package version in CI
`pip install openai` | Official Python SDK | https://pypi.org/project/openai/
`pip install tiktoken` | Token counting for cost estimation | https://pypi.org/project/tiktoken/
`pip install tenacity` | Retry with exponential backoff | https://pypi.org/project/tenacity/
`pip install pydantic` | Structured output validation | https://pypi.org/project/pydantic/
`openai api` | CLI for quick completions and file uploads | bundled with `openai` package
OpenAI Platform | SaaS | Yes | Primary API; `OPENAI_API_KEY` env var; usage limits per tier
Azure OpenAI | SaaS | Yes | Same API surface; enterprise compliance; requires Azure subscription
LiteLLM | OSS | Yes | Drop-in proxy; route `openai` SDK calls to Claude/Gemini without code changes
Langfuse | OSS/SaaS | Yes | Trace + log all OpenAI calls; self-hostable
Helicone | SaaS | Yes | Proxy with caching, rate limiting, analytics; minimal code change

checklist.check: SDK Installation: Install openai Python package (pip install openai)
SDK Installation: Install Node.js SDK for JavaScript (npm install openai)
SDK Installation: Verify SDK version compatibility with API
SDK Installation: Create virtual environment for Python projects
SDK Installation: Add SDK to requirements.txt or package.json
SDK Installation: Test SDK import and initialization
API Key Configuration: Set OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable
API Key Configuration: Verify API key validity and permissions
API Key Configuration: Use API key from OpenAI dashboard
API Key Configuration: Store keys securely (never hardcode)
API Key Configuration: Implement key rotation strategy
API Key Configuration: Test authentication with simple API call
Client Initialization: Create OpenAI client with API key
Client Initialization: Configure client for Python or JavaScript
Client Initialization: Set up error handling for client creation
Client Initialization: Test API connectivity
Client Initialization: Document client configuration
Client Initialization: Implement client as singleton/shared instance
Model Selection: Review available models (gpt-4o, gpt-4o-mini, etc.)
Model Selection: Understand model capabilities and costs
Model Selection: Select appropriate model for use case
Model Selection: Document model selection rationale
Model Selection: Plan for model updates/deprecation
Model Selection: Test multiple models if needed
Request Creation: Build messages array with role/content
Request Creation: Set max_tokens parameter
Request Creation: Configure temperature for randomness control
Request Creation: Add optional parameters (top_p, top_k, etc.)
Request Creation: Implement request timeout handling
Request Creation: Test request creation and validation
Response Handling: Parse response.choices[0].message.content
Response Handling: Extract finish_reason (stop, length, content_filter, tool_calls)
Response Handling: Track usage tokens (prompt, completion, total)
Response Handling: Handle partial responses
Response Handling: Implement response validation
Response Handling: Log responses for debugging
Error Handling & Recovery: Implement try/catch for API calls
Error Handling & Recovery: Handle authentication errors (401)
Error Handling & Recovery: Handle rate limit errors (429) with backoff
Error Handling & Recovery: Handle server errors (5xx) with retry
Error Handling & Recovery: Implement exponential backoff strategy
Error Handling & Recovery: Log detailed error information
Error Handling & Recovery: Provide fallback responses
Advanced Features: Implement streaming responses
Advanced Features: Support vision/image inputs if needed
Advanced Features: Implement function calling (if applicable)
Advanced Features: Support batch processing
Advanced Features: Implement request caching if appropriate
Advanced Features: Handle long context sequences
Production Deployment: Implement monitoring for API calls
Production Deployment: Set up alerting for failures
Production Deployment: Implement rate limiting client-side
Production Deployment: Configure API key rotation
Production Deployment: Set up cost tracking and budgeting
Production Deployment: Test failover scenarios
Production Deployment: Implement graceful degradation
Production Deployment: Document API dependencies and SLAs
